summary.en: Amarillo's historic and civic heart, a landscape of classic early 20th-century architecture, modern cultural institutions, and the ever-present, impossibly wide Texas sky.
A district in transition focused on large-scale entertainment and public art, this area serves as the city's central gathering place.
An unassuming culinary haven where a vibrant cluster of family-run Asian markets offers authentic flavors from around the world.

name.en: Globe-News Center for the Performing Arts

description.en: A state-of-the-art venue with a sleek, modern design that hosts the Amarillo Symphony and Opera, symbolizing the city's commitment to the arts.
This modern ballpark, home to the Amarillo Sod Poodles baseball team, is the epicenter of local fun and a symbol of downtown revitalization.
A city-wide public art project of bizarre and poetic street signs. Downtown and SOPO are the perfect places to start the ultimate urban scavenger hunt to find them.
The real draw for a day-tripper is the authentic, home-style hot food served here on Saturdays, offering dishes not found on typical restaurant menus.
An unexpected splash of color and beauty inside the market, this flower bar contributes to the store's calming and peaceful atmosphere.

prompt.en: Find a spot where a historic, early 20th-century building stands next to a modern one. Capture both in a single photo.
Take a photo looking straight up the facade of a tall building, capturing as much of the impossibly wide Texas sky as possible.
Find a symbol of civic pride—like a city flag, a public monument, or an official seal on a building—and take a close-up shot.
Get close to an old brick or stone building and take a photo that focuses on the texture and craftsmanship of the past.
Find a modern glass window and capture the reflection of a historic building in it.
Take a photo from a low angle that tells a story about the downtown streets. Focus on details like street signs, manhole covers, or storefronts.
This area is more about civic institutions than bustling retail. Find a quiet corner or empty plaza and capture the sense of space.
Capture an interesting pattern made by the shadows of downtown buildings on the sidewalk or on another building.
Take a selfie with what you think is the most impressive piece of architecture in the background.
Photograph a vintage-looking sign on a building, whether it's neon, painted, or carved in stone.
Find a poster or advertisement for a local arts event (symphony, opera, theatre) and take a creative photo of it.
Create a mini photo collage (3 pictures) of the dominant colors you see in the neighborhood: brick red, sky blue, and something unexpected.

title.en: Art Where You Least Expect It

prompt.en: Find a piece of public art, whether it's a sculpture, a mural, or one of the famous Dynamite Museum signs. Frame it with its urban surroundings.
Capture the feeling of entertainment and local fun. Photograph something that represents sports or performance culture in the area.
This neighborhood is 'in transition.' Take a photo that shows this mix of old and new, like a modern structure next to an empty lot or an older building.
The Sod Poodle (a prairie dog) is the local mascot. Find any depiction of a prairie dog or another local animal and snap a picture.
Imagine you're heading to a game. Find a local bar or eatery that looks like a great pre-game watering hole and photograph its storefront.
Channeling the Dynamite Museum, find the most unusual or poetic-sounding street sign or business name and take a photo.
Take a selfie in a spot that looks like a central gathering place for the city, like a plaza or the entrance to a major venue.
Use an architectural feature, like an archway or a window, to frame a shot of a landmark in the distance.
If you're here in the late afternoon, capture a building facade lit up by the warm light of the 'golden hour' just before sunset.
Take a photo down a long, straight street that emphasizes the wide-open grid layout of the city.
Take a 10-second video of the ambient sounds of the neighborhood. Is it traffic, distant cheers, or quiet?
Find an example of modern construction that uses brick and steel and take a photo that highlights the interplay between these two materials.
Find a product in a market with packaging in a language you don't recognize. Take an artistic photo of it.
Photograph the most colorful display of fresh produce or packaged goods you can find in one of the international markets.
Find an interesting-looking candy, snack, or ice cream. Take a picture of your sweet discovery before you try it.
Take a selfie outside the most unassuming storefront that you think hides the most interesting things inside.
Take a photo looking down a long market aisle, capturing the sheer variety of products available.
Find a fruit, vegetable, or sauce you've never seen before. Take a close-up shot and try to guess what it tastes like.
Capture a photo that shows the contrast between the typical American suburban landscape and the vibrant international culture found here.
Find the spice aisle or fresh herbs section in a market. Take a photo and describe the most interesting smell.
Some markets here are praised for their calm atmosphere. Find a quiet corner or a beautifully organized shelf and capture that sense of peace.
Look for signs of a family-run business—a handwritten sign, a unique display, or a friendly interaction. Capture the essence of it in a photo.
Find the beverage cooler in a market and take a photo of the most interesting-looking drink you can find.
Take a screenshot of your map app showing the pins of at least two different international markets you've visited or located in the area.
